description_long: This object is a stylized tombstone made of gray stone, featuring a multi-tiered rectangular base and a tall, arched headstone. The front face of the headstone is adorned with a carved skull and crossbones above the large, raised letters 'RIP', and several visible cracks add to its aged appearance. The stone has a matte, slightly rough finish, with subtle shading that suggests weathering. The sides of the tombstone are plain and show the thickness of the stone, while the back is unadorned and smooth. The overall form is symmetrical, with the headstone rising vertically from the center of the stepped base. This type of tombstone is commonly found in graveyards or cemeteries, often used as a grave marker.
description: A gray stone tombstone with a stepped base, arched headstone, and 'RIP' inscription with a skull and crossbones on the front.
description_view_0: Front view showing the plain, arched headstone and stepped base without any inscriptions or symbols.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thickness of the headstone and the layered structure of the base.
description_view_2: Front view with visible 'RIP' inscription, skull and crossbones, and cracks on the headstone.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the thickness and profile of the tombstone.
